# UC-22.14.32 · Re-Authentication for Sensitive Application Roles

## Description

Measures time since last strong authentication for high-risk app roles supporting IA-11 re-authentication requirements.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NIST 800-53 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes o365 and sourcetypes ms:o365:management. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Replace ApplicationId tokens with production GUIDs; (2) align session length to policy; (3) pair with CASB step-up events if available; (4) document exceptions for legacy apps. Indexes required: `index=o365`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=ms:o365:management`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (Splunkbase 4055).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `ms:o365:management` sign-ins with `AuthenticationRequirement` / `SessionId` if present.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Replace ApplicationId tokens with production GUIDs; (2) align session length to policy; (3) pair with CASB step-up events if available; (4) document exceptions for legacy apps.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=o365 sourcetype=ms:o365:management earliest=-24h
| search ApplicationId IN ("*high_risk_app_guid_1*","*high_risk_app_guid_2*")
| stats earliest(_time) as first_seen, latest(_time) as last_seen by UserId, SessionId
| eval session_age_min=round((now()-last_seen)/60,1)
| where session_age_min>480
```

Understanding this SPL

**Re-Authentication for Sensitive Application Roles (IA-11)** — Measures time since last strong authentication for high-risk app roles supporting IA-11 re-authentication requirements.

Documented **Data sources**: `ms:o365:management` sign-ins with `AuthenticationRequirement` / `SessionId` if present. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (Splunkbase 4055). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: o365; **sourcetype**: ms:o365:management. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=o365, sourcetype=ms:o365:management, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Applies an explicit `search` filter to narrow the current result set.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by UserId, SessionId** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **session_age_min** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where session_age_min>480` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare a sample of the same events in the source system (domain controller, cloud tenant, or device) the security team would open first. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (long sessions), Single value (policy breaches), Timeline

## SPL

```spl
index=o365 sourcetype=ms:o365:management earliest=-24h
| search ApplicationId IN ("*high_risk_app_guid_1*","*high_risk_app_guid_2*")
| stats earliest(_time) as first_seen, latest(_time) as last_seen by UserId, SessionId
| eval session_age_min=round((now()-last_seen)/60,1)
| where session_age_min>480
```

## Visualization

Table (long sessions), Single value (policy breaches), Timeline. Key fields: `SessionId`, `UserId`, `first_seen`, `last_seen`, `session_age_min`.
